title: heatwave in Uttar Pradesh pushes temperatures up to 46°C, Moradabad residents face severe challenges - The News Mill
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Uttar Pradesh
full_text:
Uttar Pradesh is experiencing severe heatwave conditions, with temperatures ranging from 43°C to 46°C across multiple districts, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) reported on Sunday, May 24, 2026. The heatwave, including severe heat conditions, is expected to persist particularly in eastern parts of the state.
Residents of Moradabad described worsening conditions as intense heat combined with rising humidity has made daily life difficult. Many are venturing outdoors only for essential activities, relying on cold beverages and water to find relief.
Akhil Khan, who went out to purchase medicine for his child, stated, “It is extremely hot today. Stepping out on the road is becoming very difficult. It was necessary to come out, otherwise I would have stayed at home.”
Khilendra, another local resident, added, “It’s very hot, and the humidity is extremely high. We mostly stay indoors and only come out for urgent necessities. Otherwise, we finish our work in the morning or evening.”
Advice to take precautions was echoed by Ashok Mittal, who said, “The temperature has risen significantly today, causing a lot of difficulty. In this heat, one should drink plenty of water, take care and avoid stepping out in the sun unless it is essential.”
At several roadside locations in Moradabad, people were observed stopping to receive sharbat distributed outside a Gurdwara to provide relief from the heat. Gurjit Singh explained, “Due to the extreme heat, the Gurdwara is distributing sharbat to provide some relief. The temperature is at its peak this season, which is why we are serving it.”
A passerby praised the initiative, saying, “The heat is intense and the sun is scorching. This arrangement for distributing sherbet is a very good initiative. People are stopping to drink it to get some relief.”
Meanwhile, Congress leader Supriya Shrinate criticised the Uttar Pradesh government over power cuts during the heatwave, highlighting widespread distress among residents. She stated, “It’s unbearably hot, but in Uttar Pradesh, the people are forced to endure hell–nowhere else are there such severe power cuts.” She added, “From Meerut to Ballia, people are distressed. Children, the elderly, women and farmers are all suffering in this scorching heat.”
The IMD has advised residents to avoid prolonged direct sunlight exposure, stay hydrated and limit outdoor activities during peak afternoon hours.

title: Uttar Pradesh heatwave alert: State reels under severe heat; Moradabad residents struggle as temperatures soar up to 46°C - Zee News
full_text:
Uttar Pradesh heatwave alert: State reels under severe heat; Moradabad residents struggle as temperatures soar up to 46°C
In Moradabad, residents described worsening conditions due to intense heat and rising humidity, saying daily life has been severely affected as people step out only for essential work and rely on cold beverages and water for relief.
Trending Photos
MORADABAD: Uttar Pradesh continued to reel under severe heatwave conditions on Sunday, with temperatures ranging between 43 degrees Celsius and 46 degrees Celsius across several districts, according to the India Meteorological Department (IMD). Heatwave to severe heatwave conditions are likely to persist over parts of the state, particularly in eastern Uttar Pradesh.
In Moradabad, residents described worsening conditions due to intense heat and rising humidity, saying daily life has been severely affected as people step out only for essential work and rely on cold beverages and water for relief. "It is extremely hot today. Stepping out on the road is becoming very difficult," said Akhil Khan, who had stepped out to buy medicine for his child. "It was necessary to come out, otherwise I would have stayed at home."
Another resident, Khilendra, said humidity levels had further aggravated the situation, making it difficult to remain outdoors for long durations. "It's very hot, and the humidity is extremely high. We mostly stay indoors and only come out for urgent necessities. Otherwise, we finish our work in the morning or evening," he said.
Resident Ashok Mittal also urged people to take precautions amid the extreme weather conditions and avoid unnecessary exposure to the sun. "The temperature has risen significantly today, causing a lot of difficulty. In this heat, one should drink plenty of water, take care and avoid stepping out in the sun unless it is essential," he said.
At several locations in Moradabad, people were seen stopping at roadside stalls distributing sharbat to get relief from the scorching heat. Gurjit Singh said the initiative was being carried out outside a Gurdwara for passersby. "Due to the extreme heat, the Gurdwara is distributing sharbat to provide some relief. The temperature is at its peak this season, which is why we are serving it," he said.
Another passerby appreciated the effort, saying such initiatives help people cope with extreme weather conditions. "The heat is intense and the sun is scorching. This arrangement for distributing sherbet is a very good initiative. People are stopping to drink it to get some relief," he said.
Meanwhile, Congress leader Supriya Shrinate criticised the Uttar Pradesh government over power cuts amid the ongoing heatwave, alleging widespread distress among residents.
"It's unbearably hot, but in Uttar Pradesh, the people are forced to endure hell--nowhere else are there such severe power cuts," Shrinate said, adding, "From Meerut to Ballia, people are distressed. Children, the elderly, women and farmers are all suffering in this scorching heat." The IMD has advised people to avoid prolonged exposure to direct sunlight, stay hydrated and limit outdoor activity during peak afternoon hours.

Ballia News: तापमान 40 डिग्री पार, लू का अलर्ट
विज्ञापन
खबरें लगातार पढ़ने के लिए अमर उजाला एप डाउनलोड करें
या
वेबसाइट पर पढ़ना जारी रखने के लिए वीडियो विज्ञापन देखें
अगर आपके पास प्रीमियम मेंबरशिप है तो
विज्ञापन
बलिया। चार दिनों से जिले में पुरवा हवा की उमस से शनिवार को राहत मिली। सुबह पुरवा हवा से उमस बना रहा लेकिन दिन चढ़ने के साथ ही पश्चिमी हवा का रुख हो गया। इस दौरान सूर्य की तीखी किरणें आग उगलती रही लेकिन लोगों को उमस भरी गर्मी से राहत मिली। इस दौरान लू का असर बना रहा। शाम को आसमान में बादल के हवा चलने से लोगों को उमस से निजात मिली और लोग काम काज में जुटे रहे। चार दिनों से उमस भरी भीषण गर्मी ने जन जीवन पूरी तरह से बेहाल हो गया है। पशु पक्षी भी परेशान दिखाई दे रहे थे। पश्चिमी हवा से लू का असर बना रहा। दोपहर में सड़कों पर सन्नाटा पसरा रहा। लोग गमछा व टोपी लगाकर ही घरों से बाहर निकले। सूर्य की किरणें आग उगल रही थी। वहीं सड़कों से आंच निकल रहा था। इधर एक सप्ताह से अधिक समय से अधिकतम तापमान 40 डिग्री पार रहने से प्रशासन ने लू का अलर्ट जारी किया था। शनिवार को अधिकतम तापमान 41.2 व न्यूनतम 28 डिग्री सेल्सियस दर्ज किया गया। गांधी महाविद्यालय के मौसम विशेषज्ञ प्रो. सुरेश सिंह ने बताया कि हवा के रुख में बदलाव हुआ है। लू का असर बना रहेगा। पश्चिमी हवा बहने से दिन में तपिश रहेगी लेकिन रात को मौसम में ठंडक बना रहेगा। अभी एक सप्ताह तक मौसम इसी तरह बना रहेगा।
-- --
लू के लक्षण
लू के प्रमुख लक्षण गर्म और लाल त्वचा, पसीना न आना, तेज नाड़ी, सांस लेने में तेजी, चक्कर आना, सिरदर्द, कमजोरी, उल्टी जैसा महसूस होना, भ्रम की स्थिति तथा पेशाब कम होना हीट स्ट्रोक के मुख्य लक्षण हैं। एडीएम ने ऐसी स्थिति में तुरंत चिकित्सकीय सहायता लेने का निर्देश दिया है।
-- --
लू से बचने के उपाय
- प्यास न लगने पर भी हर 20-30 मिनट में पानी पीते रहें। नींबू पानी, छाछ, नारियल पानी और शिकंजी का नियमित सेवन करें।
-हल्का और ताजा भोजन करें। तरबूज, खरबूजा, ककड़ी और खीरा जैसी पानी से भरपूर चीजों को अपनी डाइट में शामिल करें।
- बाहर निकलते समय पूरे शरीर को ढकने वाले हल्के रंग के और ढीले सूती कपड़े पहनें।
- तेज धूप में बाहर निकलते समय सिर और चेहरे को गमछे या टोपी से अच्छी तरह ढकें। आंखों को बचाने के लिए धूप के चश्मे पहने।
-आप धूप में काम करते हैं, तो बीच-बीच में छाया में जाकर आराम करें और शरीर को ठंडा रखें।
